basics.name: Neel Shah
basics.label: Robotics PhD Student
basics.summary: Robotics PhD student at Georgia Tech focused on sensor fusion, state estimation, and learning-based control for highly dynamic systems.

work.name: Stebner Labs
work.summary: Research in real-time sensing, estimation, and control for highly dynamic systems.
highlights: Designed a nonlinear Kalman filter for real-time sensor fusion and state estimation.
Deployed a deep RL agent for robust feed-forward control of nonlinear high-delay dynamics.
Engineering a multimodal sensor array using Bayesian inference and ML-based fusion for signal processing.
Managed the Rapid Prototyping and Electronics Lab; trained 55 undergraduates and mentored 4.

work.name: Los Alamos National Laboratory
work.summary: Research in autonomous inspection, uncertainty quantification, and 3D perception pipelines.
highlights: Invented a deformable convolution workflow for anomaly detection and wavenumber estimation in curved geometries.
Performed theoretical uncertainty quantification for sensor systems to improve reliability in autonomous inspection.
Engineered automated 3D point cloud registration pipelines using Open3D for arbitrary LiDAR data.

work.name: VitalEyes
work.summary: Built software to help research labs proactively manage capacity and social distancing.
highlights: Led customer discovery by interviewing industry experts and building relationships in academia.
Managed the tech stack, unit tests, and release workflows; launched across 5 labs with 30+ researchers.

volunteer.summary: Developing STEM enrichment content for underserved communities.
highlights: Collaborating with Atlanta Mission Women’s Shelter to develop STEM enrichment courses for unhoused children.
Partnered with Agape community outreach to develop math skills in underserved K-12 students.

education.institution: Thomas Jefferson High School for Science and Technology

awards.title: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program (GRFP)
awards.summary: Graduate research fellowship supporting doctoral studies in robotics.

publications.name: General Curved Surface Full-field Thickness Estimation utilizing Deformable Kernels for Wavenumber Filtering
publications.publisher: Journal manuscript in progress (targeting Mechanical Systems and Signal Processing)
publications.summary: Journal manuscript and patent in progress.

publications.name: Uncertainty Quantification of Thickness Estimation via Full-field Ultrasonic Inspection
publications.publisher: International Workshop for Structural Health Monitoring (IWSHM) 2023; AIVELA 2025
publications.summary: Published and presented work on uncertainty quantification for full-field ultrasonic thickness estimation.

projects.name: Vision-Language Foundation Models (CS 8803 Final Project)
projects.summary: Out-of-distribution uncertainty quantification and inference optimization for OpenVLA.
highlights: Implemented Bayesian LoRA and Laplace approximation for OpenVLA OOD uncertainty quantification.
Optimized inference to 99.2% success on LIBERO via confidence-based dynamic allocation of open-loop actions.
